weather_report:
Osborne Park wakes to a brushed-blue ceiling with not a stitch of cloud, the light already clean and insistent. Clarity is excellent; edges feel sharper, colors a touch more vivid, and shadows lie with purpose. Expect the sun to stay dominant through the day, with the UV index climbing quickly toward high around the middle hours, so the kind of brightness that flatters everything can still be unkind to unguarded skin. It looks like a plainly beautiful day, more gentle than summer but still assertive.

Skies hold fair through the afternoon, the air likely warming enough for comfort in short sleeves, though a light layer won’t go astray if you linger in shade. Sunglasses, a brimmed hat, and sunscreen will make the difference between glowing and glowing too much. This is a good morning to wander to a café with outdoor tables tucked under an awning, run small errands on foot, or hang laundry while the sun does its quiet work. Later, take a slow loop through a nearby park and read a few pages on a bench, moving with the sun as it arcs, untroubled, across the sky.
